data.title: Vince Holding names Yuji Okumura CFO
data.description:
• Vince Holding (NYSE:VNCE) said on Tuesday that Yuji Okumura, the company's interim Chief Financial Officer since March 2025 and Controller since September 2020, has been named Chief Financial Officer, effective April 14, 2025.
  • Okumura has nearly two decades of experience in accounting and financial reporting. He has served as the company’s Vice President, Controller since 2020 after originally joining the organization in 2018 as its Director of Financial Reporting. 
  • Prior to Vince Holding, Okumura worked for over 11 years in public accounting at KPMG US. 
  • Vince Holding (NYSE:VNCE) shares were up 5.59% in pre-market trading.

data.title: Colliers to acquire Canadian commercial real estate services platform, Triovest
data.description:
• Colliers (NASDAQ:CIGI) said on Tuesday that it has entered into a definitive agreement to acquire Triovest, a Canadian commercial real estate services platform, from its owner, Coril Holdings.
  • After closing, Triovest will rebrand as Colliers, merging its operations and capabilities to solidify Colliers’ position as Canada’s largest commercial real estate services firm. 
  • The combined Canadian operations will employ more than 3,000 professionals, manage more than 95 million square feet of commercial real estate, and oversee more than $15 billion in projects under development. Terms of the transaction were not disclosed.
  • Colliers (NASDAQ:CIGI) shares were down 0.60% in extended trading.

data.title: Lazard announces strategic expansion into the UAE
data.description:
Financial advisory and asset management firm Lazard (NYSE:LAZ) announced on Tuesday a strategic expansion into the United Arab Emirates, with Abu Dhabi becoming the financial advisory main office for the country.

Hussain Altajir is appointed CEO of Lazard Financial Advisory in the UAE.

data.title: Funko plans to expand to Southeast Asia with a store in the Philippines
data.description:
Funko (FNK) announced on Tuesday it will open its first Southeast Asian Funko-licensed store in the Philippines through a partnership with local retailer Funtastik Enterprises Corp. The store is set to debut in June 2025 at SM Mall of Asia, one of the region’s largest shopping destinations. 

The store will feature exclusive product releases and interactive zones, celebrating a wide range of entertainment, sports, and music franchises, as well as the growing world of anime. Shoppers can expect to find a variety of collectibles, including the iconic Funko Pop! line, Bitty Pop!, Loungefly, and Manila-exclusive apparel.

The toy company said the expansion is a significant step in its international growth strategy, reflecting the commitment to bringing its brand experience closer to its most passionate communities. The Philippines was chosen by Funko (FNK) due to its vibrant pop culture scene, characterized by thriving comic conventions, strong community engagement, and a rapidly expanding e-commerce ecosystem. 

Notably, Filipino fans are among the world's most engaged Funko (NASDAQ:FNKO) collectors, making the market a strategic choice for further local engagement and fanbase expansion. Funko (NASDAQ:FNKO) CEO Cynthia Williams emphasized that the Philippines is one of the company’s strongest-performing markets in Asia and that the new store represents a direct investment in its dedicated fan community. 

With the launch, Funko (FNKO) said it aims to accelerate its global reach and shape the next era of fan culture in Asia and beyond, leveraging best-in-class retail and licensing partnerships to inspire connection, self-expression, and fun among fans worldwide.

Shares of Funk (FNKO) moved 0.5% higher in premarket trading to $4.27 vs. the 52-week range of $4.18 to $14.65.

data.title: Church & Dwight is called a consumer staples standout by BofA
data.description:
Church & Dwight Co. (NYSE:CHD) gained in early trading on Tuesday after Bank of America upgraded the household products stocks to a Buy rating from Neutral and called it a standout in the consumer staples sector.

The positive view is that Church & Dwight (NYSE:CHD) is poised to benefit from a challenging consumer backdrop and potential trade-down with its value portfolio.

The firm also sees a compelling risk/reward setup due to improving volumes with consistent market share gains, limited private label risk to the value portfolio, potential for tuck-in M&A, and a history of outperformance in economic downturns.  

Analyst Anna Lizzul highlighted CHD's exposure to value products while continuing to elevate the premium portfolio through innovation

"We view CHD’s sales portfolio mix with 36% value and 64% premium, and ~82% US sales, as well positioned in a recessionary environment. CHD’s portfolio has a combination of improving volume trends and market share gains with low risk to private label, as only five of CHD’s 18 categories have private label exposure."

Bank of America assigned a price objective of $30 to CHD based on a 2026 P/E ratio of 30X. The premium valuation was said to be an appropriate premium for consistent volume growth supported by innovation andlow risk to private label, with minimal risk to tariff impact. 

Shares of Church & Dwight Co. (CHD) were up 1.0% in premarket action to $106.58 vs. the 52-week range of $96.35 to $116.46.

data.title: Albertsons Companies declares $0.15 dividend
data.description:
• Albertsons Companies (NYSE:ACI) declares $0.15/share quarterly dividend, in line with previous.
  • Forward yield 2.78%
  • Payable May 9; for shareholders of record April 25; ex-div April 25.
  • See ACI Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.

data.title: Bank of America Q1 earnings beat as net interest income, sales and trading revenue rise
data.description:
Bank of America (NYSE:BAC) stock gained 2.1% in Tuesday premarket trading after the bank turned in a bottom-line and top-line beats, helped by sales and trading revenue growth at its Global Markets division.

The bank's year-over-year 18% increase in GAAP EPS "reflected growth in net interest income and fee income, while sales and trading delivered its 12th consecutive quarter of year-over-year revenue growth," said Chairman and CEO Brian Moynihan.

Bank of America (NYSE:BAC) expects Q4 net interest income on fully taxable equivalent basis of $15.5B-$15.7B (vs. Visible Alpha consensus of $15.6B), up from $14.6B in Q1.

Q1 GAAP EPS of $0.90, beating the average analyst estimate of $0.82, rose from $0.82 in Q4 2024 and $0.76 in last year’s Q1.

Net interest income (FTE basis) of $14.6B, matching the $14.6B Visible Alpha estimate, increased from $14.5B in the prior quarter and $14.2B a year ago.

Noninterest income of $12.9B, topping the $12.4B VA consensus, climbed from $11.0B in Q4 and $11.8B in last year’s Q1.

Provision for credit losses rose to $1.48B from $1.45B in the previous quarter and $1.32B a year ago.

Q1 noninterest expense of $17.8B grew from $16.8B in Q4 and $17.2B in Q1 2024.

Bank of America’s (BAC) return on average tangible common shareholders’ equity was 13.9% vs.12.6% in the previous quarter and 12.7% a year ago.

Average deposits totaled $1.96T, about even with Q4. Average loans of $1.90T edged up from $1.08T in the prior quarter.

Consumer Banking revenue of $10.5B declined from $10.6B in Q4 and increased from $10.2B in last year’s Q1. Average deposits rose to $947.6B from $942.3B in Q4. Combined credit/debit card spending of $228B rose 4% Y/Y.

Global Wealth and Investment Management revenue of $6.02B climbed from $6.00B in the prior quarter and $5.59B a year ago. Net income of $1.01B dropped from $1.17B in Q4 and was roughly flat with Q1 2024. Client balances of $4.2T increased 5% Y/Y, driven by positive net client flows and higher market valuations.

Global Banking revenue of $5.98B vs. $6.08B in the prior quarter and $5.98B a year ago. Net income of $1.91B decreased from $2.14B in Q4 and $1.99B in last year’s Q1. Average deposits of $575.2B rose 9% Y/Y and average loans and leases of $378.7B increased 1% Y/Y.

Global Markets revenue (ex-net DVA) of $6.57B grew from $4.86B in Q4 and $5.97B in Q1 2024. Net income (ex-DVA) was $1.94B vs. $955M in the prior quarter and $1.79B a year ago. Sales and trading revenue, excluding net DVA, of $5.7B climbed 9% Y/Y.

Conference call at 8:30 AM ET.

Earlier, Bank of America GAAP EPS of $0l90 beats by $0.08, revenue of $27.37B beats by $460M

data.title: Ivanhoe Electric poised for $825M financing from Export-Import Bank for Santa Cruz copper project
data.description:
Ivanhoe Electric (NYSE:IE) +5.1% pre-market Tuesday after saying it received a letter of interest from the Export-Import Bank of the United States outlining the potential to provide up to $825M in debt financing for the development of the Santa Cruz copper project in Arizona.

The project aligns with the EXIM Bank's Make More in America initiative, which is intended to increase U.S. supply of critical minerals to foster economic growth and strengthen national security through domestic resource production, the company said.

Ivanhoe Electric (NYSE:IE) said is assessing EXIM Bank's interest together with other financing alternatives for the Santa Cruz project's development; if the company proceeds with a formal application, EXIM Bank would need to conduct all necessary due diligence to determine if a final lending commitment would be made.

The company said it remains on schedule to deliver the Santa Cruz preliminary feasibility study in June, which will support project financing activities, and the company anticipates that permits will be received and initial construction activities will begin in H1 2026.

data.title: Automotive Properties REIT acquires Florida-based automotive property for ~$13.5M
data.description:
Canada-based Automotive Properties REIT (APR.UN:CA) announced on Monday the acquisition of an automotive property in Tampa, Florida, from a third party for ~$13.5M.

The property is tenanted by the American manufacturer of electric vehicles and accessories, Rivian.

The acquisition cost was funded by drawing on the REIT's revolving credit facilities.

data.title: European markets look for direction amid hopes of tariff relief
data.description:
Update: The revised post reflects market movements as of 7.05am ET.

London (UKX) +0.83% to 8,202. The pound (GBP:USD) rose above $1.32, a six-month-high. UK's unemployment rate steady at 4.4% in February.

Germany (DAX:IND) +1.00% to 21,163, marking the second straight session of advances, as investors weighed fresh tariff developments. Germany’s wholesale prices rose by 1.3%Y/Y in March. The ZEW Indicator of Economic Sentiment for Germany sank to -14 in April 2025, the lowest since July 2023.

France (CAC:IND) +0.25% to 7,291. France inflation rate steady at 0.8% as expected.

Eurozone industrial production rose by 1.1% M/M in February. The ZEW Indicator of Economic Sentiment for the Euro Area slumped by 58.3 points from the prior month.

Slovakia's annual inflation rate increased to 4% in March.

Industrial production in Romania declined by 3.2% Y/Y in February.

Bulgaria recorded a current account deficit of €392.2M in February.

The annual inflation rate in Croatia eased to 3.2% in March.

The annual inflation rate in Poland stood at 4.9% in March.

Norway's trade surplus narrowed to NOK 60.2B in March.

The pan-European Stoxx 600 (STOXX) edged 1.01% higher to 504.30, with auto stocks in the lead. Optimism grew over a potential pause in auto tariffs for imported vehicles and parts. This will give automakers more time to expand US-based production. The auto sector led the gains, with Mercedes-Benz (OTCPK:MBGAF), BMW (NEOE:BMW:CA), Volkswagen (OTCPK:VWAGY), Stellantis (NYSE:STLA), and Renault (OTCPK:RNSDF) among the gainers. 

In the bond market, the U.S. 10-year Treasury yield was up 2 basis points to 4.38%.

Germany's 10-year yield was up 2 basis points to 2.54%.

UK's 10-year yield was up less than 1 basis point to 4.67%.

Currencies: (EUR:USD) (GBP:USD) (CHF:USD)

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:EWG), (NYSE:GF), (NYSEARCA:EWI), (NYSEARCA:EWQ), (NASDAQ:FGM), (NASDAQ:DAX), (NYSEARCA:FLGR), (NYSEARCA:FXB), (NYSEARCA:EWU), (NASDAQ:FKU), (BATS:EWUS), (NYSEARCA:FLGB), (NYSEARCA:GREK).
